sprint: Canon Update Sprint -- Traveller Gateway Rework V1.1
task: A frozen product-canon correction delivered as a complete specification, superseding the just-shipped 'gate to known-login-only' behaviour: Traveller Journey (WHO is arriving) is now explicitly separated from Traveller Gateway (the physical Sky-to-Hall cinematic journey, which EVERY person experiences, both a Returning Creator and a first-time Traveller). js/app.js's bootstrapSession() now calls GatewaySequence.begin() unconditionally on every boot and hands off straight into _beginBoot(), never back through the standalone Identity Gate -- checkIdentityGate() survives only as _afterGateway()'s own defensive fallback. Six scenes, always in order: 1 (Sky) and 2 (Lumo's Arrival) unchanged; 3 (Identity) is the branch and where it ends -- a Returning Creator hears 'Welcome home... show me your stars' then is handed to a new, minimal MagicCardUI.beginCreatorSignature(card,onResult) entry point reusing the existing tap-grid challenge wholesale (the Gateway briefly, non-destructively hides itself via a new .gateway-mode-hidden-for-signature class -- never torn down -- so the lower-z-index Magic Card overlay can show through, then resumes); a declined check never blocks the Gateway, it just proceeds as a Traveller; a first-time Traveller hears the unchanged 4-line greeting. New Scene 4 (The Gates Appear -- curtain clouds part, a pure-CSS gate slowly reveals), Scene 5 (Journey Through the Sky, ~6.8s -- the gate keeps growing, Lumo flies ahead with look-back glances, extra islands/birds/particles stream past), Scene 6 (The Gates Open -- Lumo turns and raises one wing, runes twinkle, doors slide open, a full-viewport golden threshold flash fires) -- only after that does onComplete() fire, handing off directly into Studio's boot. Story Egg/bonded Companion only mount inside the Hall (CompanionDirector.init() still lives inside _beginBoot(), now reached only after the whole Gateway completes); Lumo never enters the Hall, its own DOM torn down before hand-off. A real reduced-motion gap was found and fixed: the shared playLines() helper ignored prefers-reduced-motion entirely, genuinely slow with six real scenes now -- fixed to collapse to 120ms/0ms-gap per line when reduced. Verified via a new 24-assertion Playwright suite (both paths driven end-to-end through the real UI, including tapping the actual correct pattern on the real challenge, confirming Scenes 4-6 run afterward, and confirming the correct companion mounts in the Hall -- Story Egg or the bonded companion, never Lumo either way) plus a 2-assertion fallback-surface smoke test and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing, zero regressions. Every now-stale 'only for a device with no known Magic Card' claim was corrected at its source across js/gatewaySequence.js, js/app.js, js/magicCardUI.js, index.html, css/style.css, and assets/audio/gateway/README.md.
